tr.td: Amlodipine (as amlodipine besylate) 2.5 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (2.5 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Live attenuated Human alphaherpesvirus 3 only vaccine product

ul.li: Patient will achieve and maintain blood pressure within target range through lifestyle changes as part of hypertension management.
Reduce the Apnea-Hypopnea Index (AHI) to less than 5 events per hour
Improve overall sleep quality as measured by patient-reported outcomes
Reduce daytime sleepiness as measured by the Epworth Sleepiness Scale
Achieve CPAP usage of at least 4 hours per night on 70% of nights
Lose at least 5% of baseline body weight to reduce risk of progression to diabetes
Maintain or reduce HbA1c to less than 5.7%
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg
Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week
Follow a balanced diet with reduced intake of refined carbohydrates and saturated fats
Maintain fasting blood glucose below 100 mg/dL

tr.td: Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Blood pressure panel with all children optional
Home continuous positive airway pressure unit (physical object)

text.div: Amlodipine (as amlodipine besylate) 2.5 mg oral tablet

display.value: Amlodipine (as amlodipine besylate) 2.5 mg oral tablet

text.value: Amlodipine (as amlodipine besylate) 2.5 mg oral tablet

1 tablet (2.5 mg) by mouth once daily

tetanus toxoid, combinations with diphtheria toxoid

tr.td: Live attenuated Human alphaherpesvirus 3 only vaccine product

display.value: Live attenuated Human alphaherpesvirus 3 only vaccine product

text.value: Live attenuated Human alphaherpesvirus 3 only vaccine product

ul.li: Patient will achieve and maintain blood pressure within target range through lifestyle changes as part of hypertension management.
Reduce the Apnea-Hypopnea Index (AHI) to less than 5 events per hour
Improve overall sleep quality as measured by patient-reported outcomes
Reduce daytime sleepiness as measured by the Epworth Sleepiness Scale
Achieve CPAP usage of at least 4 hours per night on 70% of nights
Lose at least 5% of baseline body weight to reduce risk of progression to diabetes
Maintain or reduce HbA1c to less than 5.7%
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg
Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week
Follow a balanced diet with reduced intake of refined carbohydrates and saturated fats
Maintain fasting blood glucose below 100 mg/dL
